name: Egypt
welcome: Hello! Welcome to Egypt — land of the pharaohs and the amazing pyramids!
summary: Egypt is one of the oldest countries in the world.
Millions of people lived here thousands of years ago — they built giant stone pyramids!
Egypt sits in northeast Africa, next to the beautiful Nile River.

LOCATION.intro: Let's see where Egypt is on our world map!

items.title: Egypt in Africa
narration: Egypt is in the northeastern corner of Africa.
It is next to the Red Sea and the Mediterranean Sea.
The mighty Nile River flows through Egypt from south to north.

LANDMARKS.intro: Egypt has some of the most amazing buildings ever made!

items.title: The Great Pyramid
narration: The Great Pyramid of Giza is one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World!
It was built over 4,500 years ago — without any machines!
More than two million stone blocks were used to build it.

items.title: The Great Sphinx
narration: The Sphinx is a giant statue with the head of a person and the body of a lion!
It has been sitting in the desert for thousands of years.
It is as tall as a six-story building!

items.title: The Nile River
narration: The Nile is the longest river in the world!
Ancient Egyptians farmed along its banks for thousands of years.
The river gave them water, food, and a way to travel.

ANIMALS.intro: Egypt is home to some fascinating animals!

items.title: Camel
narration: Camels are called the ships of the desert!
They can carry heavy loads and walk for days without water.
Their humps store fat — not water — to give them energy.

items.title: Nile Crocodile
narration: Nile crocodiles are huge reptiles that live in Egypt's rivers.
Ancient Egyptians thought they were sacred animals.
They can hold their breath underwater for up to two hours!

FOOD.intro: Egyptian food is colorful, tasty, and full of flavour!

items.title: Koshari
narration: Koshari is Egypt's national dish — everyone loves it!
It is a big bowl of rice, lentils, pasta, and crispy onions.
On top goes a spicy tomato sauce. Yummy!

items.title: Falafel
narration: Falafel are crispy little balls made from ground chickpeas.
Egyptians eat them for breakfast — stuffed inside warm bread!
They are crunchy outside and soft and green inside.

LANGUAGE.intro: In Egypt, people speak Arabic. Let's learn some words!

items.title: Marhaba — Hello
narration: Marhaba means hello in Arabic!
Say it with me — mar-HA-ba!
Arabic is read from right to left on the page.

items.title: Shukran — Thank You
narration: Shukran means thank you in Arabic!
Say it with me — SHOO-kran!
Egyptian people are very warm and friendly.

FUN_FACTS.intro: Here are some amazing facts about Egypt!

items.title: Ancient Writing
narration: Ancient Egyptians invented their own picture writing called hieroglyphics!
Each little picture was a letter or a word.
Scientists took hundreds of years to figure out how to read it.

items.title: Desert Land
narration: Almost all of Egypt is covered by the Sahara Desert — the biggest hot desert on Earth!
Only about 5% of the land is green — along the Nile River.
More than 100 million people all live in that tiny green strip!

items.title: Cat Lovers
narration: Ancient Egyptians loved cats so much, they thought they were magical!
Cats were treated like royalty and even mummified after they died.
Hurting a cat in ancient Egypt was against the law!
